body_txt: There are times when an authority figure has to be flexible but still maintain their authority. In these times, many use the following tactic to make clear that they have noticed an activity by commenting on how they haven't noticed it. This way, they're able to draw attention to the fact that the activity is prohibited while still allowing it to happen. This is not willful ignorance; the authority figure is making it known that they are aware of the situation but for some reason feel that drawing further attention to it might be a problem. This usually carries one of two messages:  Related to Could Say It, But... . See also Fake Static , Suspiciously Specific Denial , Stepping Out for a Quick Cup of Coffee and You Didn't See That . Also compare with Selective Obliviousness , where a character may pretend not to hear or notice something because they simply don't want to or know they can't deal with it.
